title: Setting Selectivity
text: ’ll start setting basic system one species two fleets, logistic selectivity fleets logistic selectivity, commerical fleet higher length initial selectivity steeper selectivity ogive. artisinal fleet, specificy units selectivity based proportions length 50% maturity (sel_unit = "p_of_mat"). form sel_start = 0.5 indicates length 50% selectivity 50% length 50% maturity, length 95% maturity length 50% maturity times sel_start + set_delta. Setting sel_unit = "p_of_mat" easier running bulk simulations. individual simulations, setting sel_unit = "length can intuitive, since selecitivy units length . , commercial case shown , length 50% selectivity set sel_start = 22 length 95% selectivity sel_start + sel_delta.
code: resolution <- 10  years <- 50  fauna <-   list(     "snapper" = create_critter(       scientific_name = "lutjanus griseus",       linf = 42,       adult_diffusion = 10,       fished_depletion = .25,       resolution = resolution     )   )   fleets <- list(   "artisanal" = create_fleet(list(     "snapper" = Metier$new(       critter = fauna$snapper,       p_explt = 0.5,       sel_unit = "p_of_mat",       sel_start = 0.5,       sel_delta = 0.1     )   ), resolution = resolution),   "commercial" = create_fleet(list(     "snapper" = Metier$new(       critter = fauna$snapper,       p_explt = 0.5,       sel_unit = "length",       sel_start = 22,       sel_delta = .01     )   ), resolution = resolution) )    sels <- data.frame(   artisinal = fleets$artisanal$metiers$snapper$sel_at_length,   commercial = fleets$commercial$metiers$snapper$sel_at_length,   length = as.numeric(colnames(fauna$snapper$length_at_age_key)) )  sels |>   pivot_longer(-length, names_to = "fleet", values_to = "selectivity") |>   ggplot(aes(length, selectivity, color = fleet)) +   geom_line()

title: Setting Selectivity
text: Contact selectivity always asymptotic; fish may become less vulnerable gears get bigger physical (e.g. longer likely get tangled nets) market reasons (released large, though users need consider whether released fish survive). common strategy model behavior -called “double normal” model, selectivity modeled using intersection two normal distributions. many types double normal functions, employ fairly simple form used Carruthers et al. 2014. parameters double normal form smallest length full selectivity smallest length 0.05 selectivity selectivity Linf case, artisinal fleet specified sel_unit = "p_of_mat" smallest length full selectivity function sel_start + sel_delta smallest length 0.05 selectivity set based sel05_anchor (case units proportions length 50% maturity). Note must less size full selectivity selectivity linf set 0, meaning largest fish longer captured gear. commerical fleet, set sel_unit = "length" values parameters adjusted accordingly. commercial fleet, selectivity Linf 0.5, meaning lower peak, zero.
code: fleets <- list(   "artisanal" = create_fleet(list(     "snapper" = Metier$new(       critter = fauna$snapper,       sel_form = "double_normal",       p_explt = 0.5,       sel_unit = "p_of_mat",       sel_start = 0.5,       sel_delta = 0.1,       sel05_anchor = 0.1,       sel_at_linf = 0     )   ), resolution = resolution),   "commercial" = create_fleet(list(     "snapper" = Metier$new(       critter = fauna$snapper,       sel_form = "double_normal",       p_explt = 0.5,       sel_unit = "length",       sel_start = 10,       sel_delta = 0.1,       sel05_anchor = 5,       sel_at_linf = 0.5     )   ), resolution = resolution) )  sels <- data.frame(   artisinal = fleets$artisanal$metiers$snapper$sel_at_length,   commercial = fleets$commercial$metiers$snapper$sel_at_length,   length = as.numeric(colnames(fauna$snapper$length_at_age_key)) )  sels |>   pivot_longer(-length, names_to = "fleet", values_to = "selectivity") |>   ggplot(aes(length, selectivity, color = fleet)) +   geom_line()

title: Setting Selectivity
text: Selectivity can also manually supplied. case ’ll pretend separately estimated selectivity commercial fleet setting sel_form = "manual" passing sel_at_age values commercial fleet.
code: commercial_selectivity <- rlnorm(length(fauna$snapper$ages))  commercial_selectivity <- commercial_selectivity / max(commercial_selectivity)  fleets <- list(   "artisanal" = create_fleet(list(     "snapper" = Metier$new(       critter = fauna$snapper,       sel_form = "double_normal",       p_explt = 0.5,       sel_unit = "p_of_mat",       sel_start = 0.5,       sel_delta = 0.1,       sel05_anchor = 0.1,       sel_at_linf = 0     )   ), resolution = resolution),   "commercial" = create_fleet(list(     "snapper" = Metier$new(       critter = fauna$snapper,       sel_form = "manual",       sel_at_age = commercial_selectivity     )   ), resolution = resolution) )   sels <- data.frame(   artisinal = fleets$artisanal$metiers$snapper$sel_at_age,   commercial = fleets$commercial$metiers$snapper$sel_at_age,   age = fauna$snapper$ages )  sels |>   pivot_longer(-age, names_to = "fleet", values_to = "selectivity") |>   ggplot(aes(age, selectivity, color = fleet)) +   geom_line()

title: Set Dynamic Habitats
text: first example, ’ll create scenario year split four seasons. simulation, bigeye tuna spend half season far “east” domain, half season concentrated spawning ground spawning occurs. added twist, can add landmasses model setting habitat value NA patches covered land. different setting habitat 0, zero value simply implies organisms don’t want live patch, land prohibits passing cells. default length habitat vectors less time steps, model treats supplied habitat list seasonal.
code: library(marlin)  library(tidyverse)  library(gganimate)  library(ggridges)  library(gifski)  years <- 20  resolution <- 10  seasons <- 4  steps <- years * seasons  time_step <- 1 / seasons  land <- expand_grid(x = 1:resolution, y = 1:resolution) %>%   filter(between(x, 7, 10) & between(y, 4, 7)) %>%   mutate(land = TRUE)   h1 <- expand_grid(x = 1:resolution, y = 1:resolution) %>%   mutate(habitat = dnorm(x, resolution / 2, .02 * resolution) * dnorm(y, resolution / 2, .02 * resolution)) %>%   mutate(habitat = habitat * (x >= 4)) %>%   left_join(land, by = c("x", "y")) %>%   mutate(habitat = ifelse(is.na(land), habitat, NA)) %>%   select(-land)    h2 <- expand_grid(x = 1:resolution, y = 1:resolution) %>%   mutate(habitat = -.5 * x + 10) %>%   mutate(habitat = habitat * (x < 4)) %>%   left_join(land, by = c("x", "y")) %>%   mutate(habitat = ifelse(is.na(land), habitat, NA)) %>%   select(-land)   bigeye_habitat <- h1 %>%   pivot_wider(names_from = x, values_from = habitat) %>%   select(-y) %>%   as.matrix()   # huh <- tidyr::pivot_longer(as.data.frame(bigeye_habitat), tidyr::everything()) # # h1$habitat2 <- as.numeric(huh$value)  bigeye_habitat2 <- h2 %>%   pivot_wider(names_from = x, values_from = habitat) %>%   select(-y) %>%   as.matrix()  recruit_habitat <- bigeye_habitat  recruit_habitat[!is.na(recruit_habitat)] <- 1  fauna <-   list(     "bigeye" = create_critter(       scientific_name = "thunnus obesus",       habitat = list(bigeye_habitat, bigeye_habitat2),       season_blocks = list(c(1, 2), c(3, 4)),       adult_home_range = c(5, 5), # standard deviation of the number of patches moved by adults       recruit_home_range = 10,       recruit_habitat = recruit_habitat,       density_dependence = "pre_dispersal",       seasons = seasons,       init_explt = 1,       explt_type = "f",       spawning_seasons = c(2, 3),       sigma_rec = 0,       ac_rec = 0,       linf = 100,       vbk = 0.2,       age_mature = 4,       m = 0.2,       weight_a = 1e-4,       weight_b = 3,       max_age = 15,       query_fishlife = FALSE     )   )   fleets <- list(   "longline" = create_fleet(     list("bigeye" = Metier$new(       critter = fauna$bigeye,       price = 10,       sel_form = "logistic",       sel_start = 1,       sel_delta = .01,       catchability = 1e-3,       p_explt = 1     )),     base_effort = 10 * resolution^2,     resolution = resolution   ) )   fleets <- tune_fleets(fauna, fleets)    spawning_ground_sim <- simmar(   fauna = fauna,   fleets = fleets,   years = years )    processed_spawning_grounds <- process_marlin(sim = spawning_ground_sim, time_step = time_step)    spawning_agg <- processed_spawning_grounds$fauna %>%   filter(age == max(age)) %>%   group_by(step) %>%   mutate(n = n / sum(n)) %>%   ungroup() %>%   ggplot(aes(x, y, fill = n)) +   geom_tile() +   transition_time(step) +   ease_aes("linear") +   scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "Tunas") +   scale_x_continuous(name = "longitude") +   scale_y_continuous(name = "latitude")   animate(spawning_agg, nframes = 100, fps = 2)

title: Set Dynamic Habitats
text: addition seasonal dynamics, may interested active range shifts species. mechanics work similarly seasonal dynamics, require vectors vector habitats equal either number years number steps (steps years times seasons per year). case, sill simulate habitat bigeye tuna changes time, first shifting east south north, moving around land mass.
code: # test habitat vector -----------------------------------------------------  shifting_habitat <- vector(mode = "list", length = years)    for (i in 1:years) {   shifting_habitat[[i]] <- expand_grid(x = 1:resolution, y = 1:resolution) %>%     mutate(habitat = -((y - (1 + i))^2) / 10) %>%     left_join(land, by = c("x", "y")) %>%     mutate(habitat = ifelse(is.na(land), habitat, NA)) %>%     select(-land) %>%     pivot_wider(names_from = x, values_from = habitat) %>%     select(-y) %>%     as.matrix() }   critter_habitat <- list(bigeye = shifting_habitat)   sim_climate <- simmar(   fauna = fauna,   fleets = fleets,   habitat = critter_habitat,   years = years )   processed_marlin <- process_marlin(sim = sim_climate, time_step = time_step, keep_age = FALSE)   patches <- expand_grid(x = 1:resolution, y = 1:resolution) |>   mutate(r0 = fauna$bigeye$r0s) |>   left_join(land)  range_shift <- processed_marlin$fauna %>%   group_by(step) %>%   mutate(n = n / sum(n)) %>%   ungroup() %>%   ggplot(aes(x, y, fill = n)) +   geom_tile() +   transition_time(step) +   ease_aes("linear") +   scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "Tunas", guide = guide_colorbar(frame.colour = "black", barwidth = unit(10, "lines"))) +   scale_x_continuous(name = "Longitude", expand = c(0, 0)) +   scale_y_continuous(name = "Latitude", expand = c(0, 0)) +   theme(legend.position = "top")   animate(range_shift, nframes = 100, fps = 4) # anim_save(filename = "ctmc.gif", animation = animate(range_shift, nframes = 100, fps = 4))

title: Manage Catch and Effort of Fleets
text: Let’s set two fleets: one open access, one constant effort. Open-access dynamics based profitability fishery require parameters, though reasonable defaults provided.

title: Manage Catch and Effort of Fleets
text: Total effort adjusts time step response normalised profitability signal: Et+1,f=Et,f×exp(ρ⋅tanh(stk))E_{t+1,f} = E_{t,f} \times \exp\left(\rho \cdot \tanh\left(\frac{s_t}{k}\right)\right) profitability signal sts_t : st=Rt−Ct|Rt|+Cts_t = \frac{R_t - C_t}{|R_t| + C_t} RtR_t CtC_t total revenue total cost previous step, respectively. signal bounded [−1,1][-1, 1], handles negative revenue safely, invariant rescaling. parameters control entry/exit dynamics: ρ\rho (oa_rho_year): annual effort adjustment rate. value 0.5 means effort can grow shrink roughly 50% per year strong profit loss signals. kk (oa_signal_half): signal value tanh response reaches half maximum. Lower values make fleets responsive. Typical range 0.15–0.4; default 0.3. oa_max_growth_per_year: maximum fractional increase effort per year highly profitable conditions (e.g. 0.5 = +50% per year). oa_max_decline_per_year: maximum fractional decrease effort per year highly unprofitable conditions (e.g. 0.5 = -50% per year). tanh function ensures : Entry/exit speed saturates extreme profits losses (avoiding unrealistic boom/bust cycles). Response approximately linear near break-even profitability. Effort converges equilibrium total profits equal zero (classic open-access outcome).

title: Manage Catch and Effort of Fleets
text: Revenue : Rt,f=∑s=1Spf,sCatchf,sR_{t,f} = \sum_{s=1}^S p_{f,s} \, \text{Catch}_{f,s} pp price Catch catch species ss fleet ff. Costs : Ct,f=c0Eref∑l=1P[(Et,l,fEref)γ+θd̃lEt,l,fEref]C_{t,f} = c_0 \, E^{\text{ref}} \sum_{l=1}^P \left[\left(\frac{E_{t,l,f}}{E^{\text{ref}}}\right)^\gamma + \theta \, \tilde{d}_l \, \frac{E_{t,l,f}}{E^{\text{ref}}}\right] : c0c_0 (cost_per_unit_effort): base cost per unit effort ErefE^{\text{ref}}: reference effort per patch (typically base_effort / n_patches) γ\gamma (effort_cost_exponent): exponent controlling congestion/convexity. Values > 1 make additional units effort progressively expensive. Default 1.2. θ\theta (travel_weight): derived internally travel_fraction d̃l\tilde{d}_l: normalised distance patch ll nearest port

title: Manage Catch and Effort of Fleets
text: Rather setting c0c_0 manually, specify target cost--revenue ratio (cr_ratio) equilibrium. value 1 means zero profits equilibrium (classic open access), > 1 means losses, < 1 means positive profits. tune_fleets(..., tune_costs = TRUE) solves c0c_0 achieves desired cr_ratio tuned depletion level. can now run simulation examine resulting fleet dynamics
code: fleets <- list(   "longline" = create_fleet(     list("bigeye" = Metier$new(         critter = fauna$bigeye,         price = 10,         sel_form = "logistic",         sel_start = 1,         sel_delta = .01,         catchability = 0,         p_explt = 2       )     ),     base_effort = resolution ^ 2,     resolution = resolution,     cr_ratio = 1,     travel_fraction = 0.5,     fleet_model = "open_access",     oa_max_growth_per_year = 0.5,     oa_max_decline_per_year = 0.5,     oa_signal_half = 0.3) , "handline" = create_fleet(   list("bigeye" = Metier$new(     critter = fauna$bigeye,     price = 10,     sel_form = "logistic",     sel_start = 1,     sel_delta = .01,     catchability = 0,     p_explt = 1   )   ),   base_effort = resolution ^ 2,   resolution = resolution,   fleet_model = "constant_effort",   cost_per_unit_effort = 2 ))  fleets <- tune_fleets(fauna, fleets, tune_type = "depletion", tune_costs = TRUE) sim <- simmar(fauna = fauna,                   fleets = fleets,                   years = years) proc_sim <- process_marlin(sim)  plot_marlin(proc_sim) proc_sim$fleets %>%    group_by(step, fleet) %>%   summarise(effort = sum(effort)) %>%    ggplot(aes(step * time_step, effort, color = fleet)) +    geom_line() +      scale_x_continuous(name = "Year")

title: Manage Catch and Effort of Fleets
text: fleet model choice determines effort responds MPA. default constant effort reallocation dynamics, total effort stays fixed redistributed inside MPA remaining fishable patches. open access model, effort responds dynamically MPA affects profitability. MPA implemented, open-access fleet initially loses access productive fishing grounds inside closure. reduces catch costs remain , driving profits triggering effort contraction. MPA matures, spillover effects (adult movement larval export protected area) can increase catch rates adjacent patches, improving profitability allowing effort partially recover. Eventually fleet settles new zero-profit equilibrium benefits spillover balanced lost access inside MPA.
code: set.seed(42) #specify some MPA locations mpa_locations <- expand_grid(x = 1:resolution, y = 1:resolution) %>% mutate(mpa = x > 4 & y < 6)  with_mpa <- simmar(fauna = fauna,                   fleets = fleets,                   years = years,                   manager = list(mpas = list(locations = mpa_locations,               mpa_year = floor(years * .5))))  proc_mpa_sim <- process_marlin(with_mpa)   proc_mpa_sim$fleets %>%    group_by(step, fleet) %>%   summarise(effort = sum(effort)) %>%    ggplot(aes(step * time_step, effort, color = fleet)) +    geom_line() +    scale_x_continuous(name = "year")

title: Manage Catch and Effort of Fleets
text: "sole_owner" fleet model identical open access effort adjustment mechanics, uses marginal profit signal rather average profit signal. causes fleet equilibrate maximum economic yield (MEY), marginal profit equals zero, rather open-access equilibrium total profit equals zero. Marginal profit computed via finite-difference perturbations (see ?calc_marginal_value). requires additional computation step, "sole_owner" fleets run slightly slower "open_access" fleets. equilibrium outcome typically features lower effort higher biomass open access, since fleet stops expanding next unit effort longer profitable, rather waiting effort unprofitable. use sole owner dynamics:
code: fleets <- list(   "longline" = create_fleet(     list("bigeye" = Metier$new(...)),     fleet_model = "sole_owner",     cr_ratio = 0.8,  # MEY typically has positive profits, so cr_ratio < 1     ...   ) )

title: Manage Catch and Effort of Fleets
text: Quotas can layered onto fleet model. impose total catch quota 8 units bigeye across fleets. quota enforced time step solving scalar effort multiplier keeps total catch quota. Important: quotas impose cap, requirement. early days fishery catches naturally exceed quota, quota binding effort reduced proportionally. However, later years population declined effort dynamics (especially open access) produce catches quota anyway, fleet unconstrained quota effect. reflects real-world quota fisheries economic biological conditions can make quotas non-binding.
code: sim_quota <- simmar(fauna = fauna,                   fleets = fleets,                   years = years,                   manager = list(quotas = list(bigeye = 8)))  proc_sim_quota <- process_marlin(sim_quota)  plot_marlin(proc_sim_quota, plot_var = "c", max_scale = FALSE) proc_sim_quota$fleets %>%    group_by(step, fleet) %>%    summarise(catch = sum(catch)) %>%    ggplot(aes(step * time_step, catch, color = fleet)) +    geom_line()+     scale_x_continuous(name = "Year") proc_sim_quota$fleets %>%    group_by(step, fleet) %>%   summarise(effort = sum(effort)) %>%    ggplot(aes(step * time_step, effort, color = fleet)) +    geom_line() +      scale_x_continuous(name = "Year")

title: Manage Catch and Effort of Fleets
text: Another management option set maximum total effort per fleet. reflect regulation (e.g. limited entry permits) physical reality (e.g. effort measured “days fished per year” fixed number vessels, natural ceiling). Set effort caps via manager = list(effort_cap = list(FLEET_NAME = EFFORT_CAP)), FLEET_NAME name fleet EFFORT_CAP maximum total effort allowed. Note: effort caps meaningful fleet_model == "open_access" "sole_owner". constant-effort fleets, effort already fixed base_effort. open-access sole-owner dynamics, cap ensures profitability signals can reduce total effort, effort can never expand beyond cap. fleet can still contract cap losses warrant , profitable conditions drive effort ceiling.
code: cap = 1.1*fleets$longline$base_effort  sim_effort <- simmar(fauna = fauna,                   fleets = fleets,                   years = years,                   manager = list(effort_cap = list(longline = cap)))  proc_sim_effort <- process_marlin(sim_effort)  plot_marlin(proc_sim_effort, plot_var = "c", max_scale = FALSE) proc_sim_effort$fleets %>%    group_by(step, fleet) %>%    summarise(catch = sum(catch)) %>%    ggplot(aes(step * time_step, catch, color = fleet)) +    geom_line()+     scale_x_continuous(name = "Year") proc_sim_effort$fleets %>%    group_by(step, fleet, patch) %>%   summarise(effort = unique(effort)) %>%    group_by(step, fleet) |>    summarise(effort = sum(effort)) |>    ggplot(aes(step * time_step, effort, color = fleet)) +    geom_line() +    geom_hline(yintercept = cap) +     scale_x_continuous(name = "Year") +    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, NA))

title: Manage Catch and Effort of Fleets
text: alternative model-driven effort dynamics, can manually specify total effort time step. Set fleet_model = "manual" provide vector effort values fleet object’s effort field. vector must length equal years * seasons. useful want impose exogenous effort trajectory — example, simulate historical expansion fishery, test effects known effort time series, explore counterfactual scenarios effort follows prescribed pattern rather responding endogenously economic signals.
code: time_steps <- years * seasons  fleets$longline$fleet_model <- "manual"  fleets$longline$effort <- fleets$longline$base_effort *  rlnorm(time_steps,0,.2)    fleets <- tune_fleets(fauna, fleets, tune_type = "depletion")  sim_effort <- simmar(   fauna = fauna,   fleets = fleets,   years = years )  proc_sim_effort <- process_marlin(sim_effort)  plot_marlin(proc_sim_effort, plot_var = "c", max_scale = FALSE) proc_sim_effort$fleets %>%   group_by(step, fleet) %>%   summarise(catch = sum(catch)) %>%   ggplot(aes(step * time_step, catch, color = fleet)) +   geom_line() +   scale_x_continuous(name = "Year") proc_sim_effort$fleets %>%   group_by(step, fleet) %>%   summarise(effort = sum(effort)) %>%   ggplot(aes(step * time_step, effort, color = fleet)) +   geom_line() +   scale_x_continuous(name = "Year")

title: Getting Started with marlin
text: Every marlin simulation needs three things: fauna — named list species (created create_critter) fleets — named list fishing fleets (created create_fleet) simmar() — function runs simulation Optionally, can also pass manager list simmar add MPAs, quotas, effort caps, let’s start without one.

title: Getting Started with marlin
text: create_critter function builds age-structured population. supply scientific_name (common_name), marlin look life history parameters FishLife database. can override parameter manually. key arguments know: resolution: size spatial grid (e.g., c(10, 10) 100 patches) seasons: number time steps per year (4 = quarterly) fished_depletion: equilibrium biomass ratio (B/B0) want fishing active. target tune_fleets calibrate . adult_home_range: distance (KM) 95% adults move source patch per year — controls mobile species density_dependence: recruitment relates space — "global_habitat" means recruits settle across whole domain weighted habitat, "local_habitat" means stay near spawned critter object built-plots inspecting life history movement:
code: resolution <- c(10, 10) years <- 20 seasons <- 4 time_step <- 1 / seasons  fauna <- list(   "bigeye" = create_critter(     common_name = "bigeye tuna",     adult_home_range = 5,     recruit_home_range = 10,     density_dependence = "local_habitat",     seasons = seasons,     fished_depletion = 0.4,     resolution = resolution,     steepness = 0.6,     ssb0 = 1000   ) ) fauna$bigeye$plot() fauna$bigeye$plot_movement()

previous_headings: A Minimal Example: One Species, One Fleet
title: Getting Started with marlin
text: Fleets built create_fleet. fleet contains one metiers — one per species interacts — specifying price, selectivity, catchability, share exploitation (p_explt). important fleet-level settings : fleet_model: "constant_effort" (total effort fixed) "open_access" (effort responds profits) spatial_allocation: effort distributed across patches — "rpue" (chase high catch rates) default base_effort: total effort units start
code: fleets <- list(   "longline" = create_fleet(     list(       "bigeye" = Metier$new(         critter = fauna$bigeye,         price = 10,         sel_form = "logistic",         sel_start = 1,         sel_delta = 0.01,         catchability = 0,         p_explt = 1       )     ),     base_effort = prod(resolution),     resolution = resolution,     fleet_model = "constant_effort"   ) )

title: Getting Started with marlin
text: process_marlin tidies raw output data frames. plot_marlin provides quick visualizations.    output process_marlin list two data frames. proc$fauna population-level results (biomass, numbers, SSB, catch patch, age, time step), proc$fleets fleet-level results (catch, revenue, effort, CPUE patch, fleet, time step). can use directly custom analyses:
code: proc <- process_marlin(sim, time_step = time_step)  # Spawning stock biomass over time plot_marlin(proc, plot_var = "ssb") # Catch over time plot_marlin(proc, plot_var = "c", max_scale = FALSE) # Spatial distribution of SSB in the final time step plot_marlin(proc, plot_var = "ssb", plot_type = "space",             steps_to_plot = max(proc$fauna$step)) proc$fleets %>%   group_by(step, fleet) %>%   summarise(catch = sum(catch), .groups = "drop") %>%   ggplot(aes(step * time_step, catch)) +   geom_line() +   labs(x = "Year", y = "Total Catch", title = "Fleet-level catch over time")

what: Scaling Up: Two Species, Two Fleets
title: Getting Started with marlin
text: Real systems multiple species multiple fleets interacting space. Let’s build realistic example spatially heterogeneous habitat.

previous_headings: Scaling Up: Two Species, Two Fleets
title: Getting Started with marlin
text: sim_habitat generates spatially correlated habitat maps multiple species simultaneously. kp parameter controls smoothness (lower = smoother), can supply correlation matrix control whether species prefer similar different areas.
code: # Species prefer different areas (negative correlation) critter_cors <- matrix(c(1, -0.5,                          -0.5, 1), nrow = 2)  habitats <- sim_habitat(   critters = c("bigeye", "skipjack"),   kp = 0.3,   critter_correlations = critter_cors,   resolution = resolution,   patch_area = 1,   output = "list" )  bigeye_habitat <- habitats$critter_distributions$bigeye skipjack_habitat <- habitats$critter_distributions$skipjack

previous_headings: Scaling Up: Two Species, Two Fleets

Pass named arguments get labeled panels:   can also compare spatial patterns:
code: # Protect a block of patches in the upper-right corner mpa_locations <- expand_grid(x = 1:resolution[1], y = 1:resolution[2]) %>%   mutate(mpa = x > 7 & y > 7)  # Visualize the MPA network ggplot(mpa_locations, aes(x, y, fill = mpa)) +   geom_tile() +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Accent", direction = -1, name = "MPA") +   coord_equal() +   labs(title = "MPA Network") sim_mpa <- simmar(   fauna = fauna,   fleets = fleets,   years = years,   manager = list(     mpas = list(       locations = mpa_locations,       mpa_year = 10     )   ) )  proc_mpa <- process_marlin(sim_mpa, time_step = time_step) plot_marlin(   "No MPA" = proc_multi,   "With MPA" = proc_mpa,   plot_var = "ssb" ) plot_marlin(   "No MPA" = proc_multi,   "With MPA" = proc_mpa,   plot_var = "c",   max_scale = FALSE ) plot_marlin(   "No MPA" = proc_multi,   "With MPA" = proc_mpa,   plot_var = "ssb",   plot_type = "space",   steps_to_plot = max(proc_mpa$fauna$step) )

title: Manual Fishing Grounds and Effort Allocation
text: ’ll first create generic snapper critter ’ll create two fleets, one allowed fish specific areas. Note one fleet, fishing grounds function identically -take MPAs. distinction fishing grounds can made fleet specific, whereas -take MPAs apply fleets, cases multiple fleets simulated specific fleets can excluded specific fishing grounds others. illustrate, ’ll create two fleets identical except fishing grounds. ’ll run simulation forward examine results, noting artisinal fleet applies fishing effort defined fishing grounds.
code: resolution <- 10 # resolution is in squared patches, so 20 implies a 20X20 system, i.e. 400 patches  years <- 50  fauna <-   list(     "snapper" = create_critter(       scientific_name = "lutjanus griseus",       adult_diffusion = 10,       fished_depletion = .5,       resolution = resolution     )   ) fishing_grounds <- expand.grid(x = 1:resolution, y = 1:resolution) |>   mutate(fishing_ground = TRUE)  fishing_grounds$fishing_ground[1:20] <- FALSE  fleets <- list(   "artisanal" = create_fleet(     list("snapper" = Metier$new(       critter = fauna$snapper,       p_explt = 1     )),     base_effort = 2 * resolution^2,     resolution = resolution,     fishing_grounds = fishing_grounds,     spatial_allocation = "rpue",     eta = 0.084   ),   "commercial" = create_fleet(     list("snapper" = Metier$new(       critter = fauna$snapper,       p_explt = 10     )),     eta = 0.084,     base_effort = 10*resolution^2,     resolution = resolution,     spatial_allocation = "ppue"   ) )  fleets <- tune_fleets(fauna, fleets, tune_type = "depletion") #> Warning: nleqslv solver did not converge (termination code: 3). Depletion #> targets may not have been achieved. Check whether target depletion is plausible #> given supplied selectivities, fishing grounds, and p_explt.  fleets$artisanal$metiers$snapper$catchability #>   artisanal  #> 0.007383882  fleets$commercial$metiers$snapper$catchability #> commercial  #> 0.01825749 fishing_ground_sim <- simmar(   fauna = fauna,   fleets = fleets,   years = years )   proc_sim <- process_marlin(fishing_ground_sim)   plot_marlin(proc_sim, plot_type = "space") #> Warning in plot_marlin(proc_sim, plot_type = "space"): Can only plot one time #> step for spatial plots, defaulting to last of the supplied steps fleet_summary <- proc_sim$fleets |>   filter(step == max(step)) |>   group_by(fleet, x, y) |>   summarise(     catch = sum(catch),     effort = sum(effort)   ) |>   mutate(cpue = catch / effort) #> `summarise()` has regrouped the output. #> ℹ Summaries were computed grouped by fleet, x, and y. #> ℹ Output is grouped by fleet and x. #> ℹ Use `summarise(.groups = "drop_last")` to silence this message. #> ℹ Use `summarise(.by = c(fleet, x, y))` for per-operation grouping #>   (`?dplyr::dplyr_by`) instead.  fleet_summary |>   ggplot(aes(x, y, fill = effort)) +   geom_tile() +   facet_wrap(~fleet) +   scale_fill_viridis_c()

title: Manual Fishing Grounds and Effort Allocation
text: alternative, users can manually specify distribution fishing effort space setting spatial_allocation = "manual" using fishing_grounds input indicate effort distributed space. key distinction manual approach simply defining fishing grounds shown previous example defining fishing grounds simply sets spatial bounds fleet’s normal spatial allocation strategy can take place, whereas setting spatial_allocation manual actually defines relative distribution effort space. example, ’ll leave commercial fleet , now specify random distribution effort artisanal fleet. Note commercial fleet’s behavior also changes result due manual distribution artisanal fleet changing distribution revenues space available commercial fleet.
code: fishing_grounds <- expand.grid(x = 1:resolution, y = 1:resolution) |>   mutate(fishing_ground = runif(length(x))) |>    mutate(fishing_ground = fishing_ground / sum(fishing_ground))    fleets <- list(   "artisanal" = create_fleet(     list("snapper" = Metier$new(       critter = fauna$snapper,       p_explt = 1     )),     base_effort = resolution^2,     resolution = resolution,     fishing_grounds = fishing_grounds,     spatial_allocation = "manual"   ),   "commercial" = create_fleet(     list("snapper" = Metier$new(       critter = fauna$snapper,       p_explt = 1     )),     base_effort = resolution^2,     resolution = resolution,     spatial_allocation = "ppue"   ) )  fleets <- tune_fleets(fauna, fleets, tune_type = "depletion")  manual_fishing_ground_sim <- simmar(   fauna = fauna,   fleets = fleets,   years = years )   proc_sim <- process_marlin(manual_fishing_ground_sim)   fleet_summary <- proc_sim$fleets |>   filter(step == max(step)) |>   group_by(fleet, x, y) |>   summarise(     catch = sum(catch),     effort = sum(effort)   ) |>   mutate(cpue = catch / effort) #> `summarise()` has regrouped the output. #> ℹ Summaries were computed grouped by fleet, x, and y. #> ℹ Output is grouped by fleet and x. #> ℹ Use `summarise(.groups = "drop_last")` to silence this message. #> ℹ Use `summarise(.by = c(fleet, x, y))` for per-operation grouping #>   (`?dplyr::dplyr_by`) instead.    fleet_summary |>   ggplot(aes(x, y, fill = effort)) +   geom_tile() +   facet_wrap(~fleet) +   scale_fill_viridis_c()

title: Setting & Understanding Movement Rates

length of one time step in the simulation in units of years  D <- (4 * patch_area) # km^2  / year grid <- tidyr::expand_grid(x = 1:resolution, y = 1:resolution)  adjacent <- grid |>   dist() |>   as.matrix()   # Mark adjacent cells adjacent[adjacent != 1] <- 0  image(adjacent) diffusion_matrix <- adjacent * D * (delta_t / delta_d^2) # adjacent times diffusion rate times simulation units  # fill in diagonal diag(diffusion_matrix) <- -1 * colSums(diffusion_matrix)  # calculate the realized diffusion over one time step of the model  grid$n <- as.numeric(n_next)  grid |>   ggplot() +   geom_tile(aes(x * delta_d, y * delta_d, fill = n)) +   ggforce::geom_circle(aes(     x0 = resolution / 2 * delta_d,     y0 = resolution / 2 * delta_d,     r = radius   )) +   scale_fill_viridis_c(limits = c(0, NA)) +   scale_x_continuous(name = "Distance from Bottom Left Corned in KM", expand = c(0, 0)) +   scale_y_continuous(name = "Distance from Bottom Left Corned in KM", expand = c(0, 0)) +   coord_fixed() #> Warning in ggforce::geom_circle(aes(x0 = resolution/2 * delta_d, y0 = resolution/2 * : All aesthetics have length 1, but the data has 400 rows. #> ℹ Please consider using `annotate()` or provide this layer with data containing #>   a single row. diffusion_frame <- data.frame(D = c(1, 20, 200))  sim_diffusion <- function(D, resolution, delta_d = 5, delta_t = 1) {   patches <- resolution^2 # number of patches    delta_d <- delta_d # length of a cell side in km    patch_area <- delta_d^2 # in units of km^2/patch    simulation_area <- patch_area * patches    grid <- tidyr::expand_grid(x = 1:resolution, y = 1:resolution)    adjacent <- grid |>     dist() |>     as.matrix()    # Mark adjacent cells   adjacent[adjacent != 1] <- 0    diffusion_matrix <- adjacent * D * (delta_t / delta_d^2) # adjacent times diffusion rate times simulation units    # fill in diagonal   diag(diffusion_matrix) <- -1 * colSums(diffusion_matrix)    # ensure matrix class   diffusion_matrix <- as.matrix(diffusion_matrix)    D_patches <- D / patch_area # translate diffusion into units of patches / year    D_patches_side <- sqrt(D_patches) # side of the diffusion box    n <- rep(0, patches)    n[grid$x == resolution / 2 & grid$y == resolution / 2] <- 100 # add 100 critters to the rough center    ytmp <- as.matrix(expm::expm(diffusion_matrix))    ytmp[ytmp < 1e-3] <- 0     radius <- sqrt(D / pi)    n_next <- n %*% as.matrix(expm::expm(diffusion_matrix)) # calculate the realized diffusion over one time step of the model    grid$n <- as.numeric(n_next)    return(grid) }  diffusion_frame <- diffusion_frame |>   dplyr::mutate(tmp = purrr::map(D, sim_diffusion, resolution = resolution, delta_d = delta_d)) diffusion_frame |>   tidyr::unnest(cols = tmp) |>   dplyr::group_by(D) |>   dplyr::mutate(n = n / max(n)) |>   ggplot() +   geom_tile(aes(x * delta_d, y * delta_d, fill = n)) +   scale_fill_viridis_c("N/max(N)", limits = c(0, NA)) +   scale_x_continuous(name = "Distance from Bottom Left Corned in KM", expand = c(0, 0)) +   scale_y_continuous(name = "Distance from Bottom Left Corned in KM", expand = c(0, 0)) +   coord_fixed() +   facet_wrap(~ round(D), labeller = label_both)

title: Spatial Allocation Strategies
text: fleet time step: 1. Flatness detection attempting optimize, algorithm checks whether objective effectively uniform across patches. range-based coefficient variation threshold (default 0.1%): CVrange=max(O)−min(O)|median(O)|<flatness_tol\text{CV}_{\text{range}} = \frac{\max(O) - \min(O)}{|\text{median}(O)|} < \text{flatness\_tol} effort distributed uniformly across open patches optimization skipped. prevents spurious concentration along edges meaningful gradient follow. 2. Standardization objective OpO_p patch pp centered scaled produce zz-score: zp=Op−median(O)scale(O)z_p = \frac{O_p - \text{median}(O)}{\text{scale}(O)} scale(O)\text{scale}(O) can median absolute deviation (default, robust), interquartile range, standard deviation. zz scores clipped [−c,c][-c, c] (default c=5c = 5) prevent extreme updates outliers: zp←max(−c,min(c,zp))z_p \leftarrow \max(-c, \min(c, z_p)) Closed patches zp=0z_p = 0. 3. Velocity field standardized objective becomes velocity removing mean across open patches: vp=zp−z‾openv_p = z_p - \bar{z}_{\text{open}} ensures velocity field integrates zero open domain, necessary conserving total effort. Patches -average objectives vp>0v_p > 0 (attracting effort), patches -average objectives vp<0v_p < 0 (repelling effort). 4. Multiplicative update Current effort epe_p updated via: epnew∝epexp(ηvp)e_p^{\text{new}} \propto e_p \exp(\eta \, v_p) η\eta (default 1.0) controls step size. proportionality constant chosen conserve total effort: epnew=epexp(ηvp)×Etotal∑pepexp(ηvp)e_p^{\text{new}} = e_p \exp(\eta \, v_p) \times \frac{E_{\text{total}}}{\sum_p e_p \exp(\eta \, v_p)} implemented log-space numerical stability. Closed patches receive epnew=0e_p^{\text{new}} = 0. 5. Optional exploration mixing eps_mix > 0, result blended uniform distribution allow re-entry temporarily abandoned patches: epfinal=(1−ϵ)epnew+ϵEtotalNopene_p^{\text{final}} = (1 - \epsilon) \, e_p^{\text{new}} + \epsilon \, \frac{E_{\text{total}}}{N_{\text{open}}}

title: Spatial Allocation Strategies
text: multiplicative update several desirable properties: Gradient-based: Effort flows “uphill” toward high-value patches along objective gradient. Effort-conserving: Total effort ∑pep\sum_p e_p exactly preserved. Non-negative: Effort become negative (provided starts non-negative). Smooth transitions: exponential update avoids discontinuous jumps, making dynamics realistic. Scale-invariant: standardization step ensures update magnitude comparable across fleets time steps regardless absolute objective values. algorithm related replicator dynamics evolutionary game theory gradient flow simplex. limit small η\eta, continuous-time equivalent : depdt=ep(vp−⟨v⟩)\frac{de_p}{dt} = e_p \left( v_p - \langle v \rangle \right) ⟨v⟩=∑p(ep/Etotal)vp\langle v \rangle = \sum_p (e_p / E_{\text{total}}) v_p effort-weighted average velocity. stable dynamical system converges Nash equilibrium effort concentrated patches highest objective values.

title: Spatial Allocation Strategies
text: ’ll use 10×10 grid two critters (bigeye tuna skipjack tuna) different spatial habitats generated sim_habitat, two fishing fleets (longline fleet open access dynamics handline fleet constant effort). Two ports placed asymmetrically create spatial cost gradients.
code: library(marlin) library(ggplot2) library(dplyr) library(tidyr)  theme_set(marlin::theme_marlin(base_size = 12) +             theme(legend.position = "top"))  resolution <- c(20, 20) patches <- prod(resolution) years <- 20 seasons <- 2 time_step <- 1 / seasons  # Two ports: one nearshore corner, one offshore ports <- data.frame(x = c(1, 8), y = c(1, 9))

title: Spatial Allocation Strategies
text: use sim_habitat generate spatially correlated habitat species. kp parameter controls spatial smoothness (lower = smoother), supply negative cross-species correlation two species prefer different parts seascape. creates spatial tradeoff: fleets maximize catch species patch.
code: # Negative correlation: species prefer different areas critter_correlations <- matrix(c(1, -0.5,                                  -0.5, 1), nrow = 2)  habitats <- sim_habitat(   critters = c("bigeye", "skipjack"),   kp = 0.1,   critter_correlations = critter_correlations,   resolution = resolution,   patch_area = 1,   output = "list" )  # Extract the habitat matrices bigeye_habitat <- habitats$critter_distributions$bigeye skipjack_habitat <- habitats$critter_distributions$skipjack  # Quick look at the generated habitats habitat_df <- expand_grid(critter = c("bigeye", "skipjack"),                           x = 1:resolution[1],                           y = 1:resolution[2]) %>%   arrange(critter, x, y) %>%   mutate(     habitat = c(as.vector(bigeye_habitat),                 as.vector(skipjack_habitat))   )  ggplot(habitat_df) +   geom_tile(aes(x, y, fill = habitat)) +   geom_point(data = ports, aes(x, y),              color = "red", size = 3, shape = 17) +   scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "D") +   facet_wrap(~critter) +   coord_equal() +   labs(title = "Underlying Habitat Quality",        subtitle = "Red triangles = port locations",        fill = "Habitat")

title: Spatial Allocation Strategies
text: keep code DRY, define helper function creates two-fleet system pair spatial_allocation settings. longline fleet operates open access (total effort responds profitability) handline fleet constant effort. fleets port locations travel_fraction 0.3, meaning travel costs comprise 30% total costs equilibrium.
code: build_fleets <- function(longline_allocation, handline_allocation) {    fleets <- list(     "longline" = create_fleet(       list(         "bigeye" = Metier$new(           critter = fauna$bigeye,           price = 10,           sel_form = "logistic",           sel_start = 1,           sel_delta = 0.01,           catchability = 0,           p_explt = 2         ),         "skipjack" = Metier$new(           critter = fauna$skipjack,           price = 5,           sel_form = "logistic",           sel_start = 0.8,           sel_delta = 0.1,           catchability = 0,           p_explt = 1         )       ),       ports = ports,       base_effort = patches,       resolution = resolution,       spatial_allocation = longline_allocation,       fleet_model = "open_access",       cr_ratio = 1,       travel_fraction = 0.7     ),     "handline" = create_fleet(       list(         "bigeye" = Metier$new(           critter = fauna$bigeye,           price = 10,           sel_form = "logistic",           sel_start = 1.2,           sel_delta = 0.1,           catchability = 0,           p_explt = 1         ),         "skipjack" = Metier$new(           critter = fauna$skipjack,           price = 8,           sel_form = "logistic",           sel_start = 0.5,           sel_delta = 0.2,           catchability = 0,           p_explt = 2         )       ),       ports = ports,       base_effort = patches,       resolution = resolution,       spatial_allocation = handline_allocation,       fleet_model = "constant_effort",       cr_ratio = 1,       travel_fraction = 0.5     )   )    fleets <- tune_fleets(fauna, fleets, tune_type = "depletion")    fleets }

title: Spatial Allocation Strategies
text: define helpers extract final-step effort biomass plot spatial maps port locations.
code: plot_effort <- function(sim, fleet_name, title = "") {   final_step <- sim[[length(sim)]]   effort_vec <- final_step[[1]]$e_p_fl[, fleet_name]    patch_effort <- expand_grid(     x = 1:resolution[1],     y = 1:resolution[2]   ) %>%     mutate(effort = effort_vec)    ggplot(patch_effort) +     geom_tile(aes(x, y, fill = effort)) +     geom_point(data = ports, aes(x, y),                color = "red", size = 3, shape = 17) +     scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "C") +     labs(title = title, fill = "Effort") +     coord_equal() +     theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 11)) }  plot_biomass <- function(proc, title = "") {   last_step <- max(proc$fauna$step)    ssb_df <- proc$fauna %>%     filter(step == last_step) %>%     group_by(critter, x, y) %>%     summarise(ssb = sum(ssb, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")    ggplot(ssb_df) +     geom_tile(aes(x, y, fill = ssb)) +     geom_point(data = ports, aes(x, y),                color = "red", size = 2, shape = 17) +     scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "B") +     facet_wrap(~critter) +     coord_equal() +     labs(title = title, fill = "SSB") +     theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 11)) }

title: Spatial Allocation Strategies
text: scenario longline fleet allocates effort based rpue (revenue per unit effort) handline fleet chases total revenue. rpue fleet moves toward patches catch rates highest per unit effort. tends spread effort evenly, high-RPUE patch attracts effort, catch rates decline relative less-fished patches. revenue fleet, contrast, gravitates toward patches highest total revenue regardless much effort already . can lead stronger concentration effort productive patches, since high effort × moderate catch rate can still produce high total revenue. Neither strategy accounts travel costs, port location directly influence fleets fish.    Notice longline fleet (rpue) distributes effort diffusely across productive patches, handline fleet (revenue) concentrates heavily highest-biomass areas. biomass maps show resulting depletion patterns: species drawn habitat overlaps fleet’s effort hotspots.
code: fleets_1 <- build_fleets("rpue", "revenue")  time_1 <- system.time({   sim_1 <- simmar(fauna = fauna, fleets = fleets_1, years = years) })  proc_1 <- process_marlin(sim_1, time_step = time_step)  plot_effort(sim_1, "longline", "Longline Effort (rpue)") plot_effort(sim_1, "handline", "Handline Effort (revenue)") plot_biomass(proc_1, "SSB — rpue vs revenue")

title: Spatial Allocation Strategies
text: Now contrast two cost-aware strategies. longline fleet uses ppue (profit per unit effort) handline fleet uses total profit. account cost traveling port, differ whether objective per-unit-effort total. ppue divides profit effort, penalizes patches effort high relative returns. profit strategy rewards patches total profit (revenue minus costs) maximized, can favor concentrating effort nearby productive patches even per-unit returns declining. travel_fraction = 0.3 two asymmetrically placed ports, see fleets pulled toward ports relative cost-naive strategies .    Compare maps Scenario 1. cost-aware fleets pulled toward port locations (red triangles), patches far port receive less effort rpue revenue. biomass maps show remote patches retain biomass cost-aware allocation, since fleets fish less intensively.
code: fleets_2 <- build_fleets("ppue", "profit")  time_2 <- system.time({   sim_2 <- simmar(fauna = fauna, fleets = fleets_2, years = years) })  proc_2 <- process_marlin(sim_2, time_step = time_step)  plot_effort(sim_2, "longline", "Longline Effort (ppue)") plot_effort(sim_2, "handline", "Handline Effort (profit)") plot_biomass(proc_2, "SSB — ppue vs profit")

title: Spatial Allocation Strategies
text: Finally, demonstrate marginal_profit, economically sophisticated allocation strategy. longline fleet now allocates based marginal return additional unit effort patch, handline fleet uses simpler rpue baseline comparison. marginal_profit, fleet responds diminishing returns: even patch high total profit high RPUE, next unit effort yield little additional return, fleet reallocates toward patches marginal returns still high. tends produce even spatial distribution effort among profit-aware strategies, directly penalizes -concentration.    marginal_profit fleet (longline) typically produces smoother effort surface cost-aware strategies. Compared rpue handline fleet sharing waters, marginal profit fleet sensitive congestion: avoids piling patches already heavily fished, even patches still high average returns. biomass maps reflect — depletion tends spatially uniform marginal_profit.
code: fleets_3 <- build_fleets("marginal_profit", "rpue")  time_3 <- system.time({   sim_3 <- simmar(fauna = fauna, fleets = fleets_3, years = years) })  proc_3 <- process_marlin(sim_3, time_step = time_step)  plot_effort(sim_3, "longline", "Longline Effort (marginal_profit)") plot_effort(sim_3, "handline", "Handline Effort (rpue)") plot_biomass(proc_3, "SSB — marginal_profit vs rpue")

text: previous scenarios varied spatial allocation strategy keeping fleet model (total effort changes time) fixed. Now flip : fleets use spatial allocation (ppue) differ fleet model. classic result fisheries economics open access fleets fish average profit zero (rents dissipated), sole owner — internalizes stock externality — stops expanding effort marginal profit next unit effort hits zero. corresponds maximum economic yield (MEY), occurs lower effort higher biomass open access equilibrium. marlin, fleet_model = "sole_owner" implements using dynamic entry/exit machinery open access, replacing profitability signal: instead responding total profits, sole owner responds effort-weighted mean patch-level marginal profits computed calc_marginal_value. build scenario manually rather using build_fleets, since fleets differ fleet_model rather spatial_allocation. Open access converge toward zero, sole owner retains positive profits equilibrium (hallmark MEY).  Finally, compare spatial effort biomass footprints end simulation.     sole owner leaves biomass water fishes less intensively. fundamental tradeoff: open access maximizes effort dissipates rents, sole owner restrains effort maximize total profit, resulting higher biomass positive economic returns.
code: fleets_oa <- list(   "longline" = create_fleet(     list(       "bigeye" = Metier$new(         critter = fauna$bigeye,         price = 10,         sel_form = "logistic",         sel_start = 1,         sel_delta = 0.01,         catchability = 0,         p_explt = 2       ),       "skipjack" = Metier$new(         critter = fauna$skipjack,         price = 5,         sel_form = "logistic",         sel_start = 0.8,         sel_delta = 0.1,         catchability = 0,         p_explt = 1       )     ),     ports = ports,     base_effort = patches,     resolution = resolution,     spatial_allocation = "ppue",     fleet_model = "open_access",     cr_ratio = 1,     travel_fraction = 0.7   ),   "handline" = create_fleet(     list(       "bigeye" = Metier$new(         critter = fauna$bigeye,         price = 10,         sel_form = "logistic",         sel_start = 1.2,         sel_delta = 0.1,         catchability = 0,         p_explt = 1       ),       "skipjack" = Metier$new(         critter = fauna$skipjack,         price = 8,         sel_form = "logistic",         sel_start = 0.5,         sel_delta = 0.2,         catchability = 0,         p_explt = 2       )     ),     ports = ports,     base_effort = patches,     resolution = resolution,     spatial_allocation = "ppue",     fleet_model = "open_access",     cr_ratio = 1,     travel_fraction = 0.5   ) )  fleets_so <- fleets_oa fleets_so$longline$fleet_model <- "sole_owner" fleets_so$handline$fleet_model <- "sole_owner"  fleets_oa <- tune_fleets(fauna, fleets_oa, tune_type = "depletion") fleets_so <- tune_fleets(fauna, fleets_so, tune_type = "depletion") years_4 <- 50  # longer run to let both fleet models reach equilibrium  time_4a <- system.time({   sim_4a <- simmar(fauna = fauna, fleets = fleets_oa, years = years_4) })  time_4b <- system.time({   sim_4b <- simmar(fauna = fauna, fleets = fleets_so, years = years_4) })  proc_4a <- process_marlin(sim_4a, time_step = time_step) proc_4b <- process_marlin(sim_4b, time_step = time_step) effort_ts <- bind_rows(   proc_4a$fleets %>%     group_by(step, fleet) %>%     summarise(effort = sum(effort), .groups = "drop") %>%     mutate(model = "open_access"),   proc_4b$fleets %>%     group_by(step, fleet) %>%     summarise(effort = sum(effort), .groups = "drop") %>%     mutate(model = "sole_owner") )  ggplot(effort_ts, aes(step * time_step, effort, color = model)) +   geom_line() +   facet_wrap(~fleet) +   scale_x_continuous(name = "Year") +   scale_y_continuous(name = "Total Effort", limits = c(0, NA)) +   labs(title = "Total Effort: Open Access vs Sole Owner",        color = "Fleet Model") profit_ts <- bind_rows(   proc_4a$fleets %>%     group_by(step, fleet) %>%     summarise(revenue = sum(revenue), catch = sum(catch), .groups = "drop") %>%     mutate(model = "open_access"),   proc_4b$fleets %>%     group_by(step, fleet) %>%     summarise(revenue = sum(revenue), catch = sum(catch), .groups = "drop") %>%     mutate(model = "sole_owner") )  ggplot(profit_ts, aes(step * time_step, revenue, color = model)) +   geom_line() +   facet_wrap(~fleet) +   scale_x_continuous(name = "Year") +   labs(title = "Revenue: Open Access vs Sole Owner",        color = "Fleet Model",        y = "Revenue") plot_effort(sim_4a, "longline", "Longline Effort (open_access)") plot_effort(sim_4b, "longline", "Longline Effort (sole_owner)") plot_biomass(proc_4a, "SSB — open_access") plot_biomass(proc_4b, "SSB — sole_owner")

title: Spatial Allocation Strategies
text: marginal_profit spatial allocation strategy sole_owner fleet model require computing numerical derivatives profit function via calc_marginal_value every time step, adds extra go_fish evaluations (one per fleet minimum). makes meaningfully slower strategies need quantities already computed standard go_fish call. Wall-clock time simmar() scenario. Sec/Year normalizes different run lengths.
code: runtime <- tibble(   scenario = c("rpue / revenue", "ppue / profit", "marginal_profit / rpue",                "open_access (ppue)", "sole_owner (ppue)"),   seconds = c(time_1["elapsed"], time_2["elapsed"], time_3["elapsed"],               time_4a["elapsed"], time_4b["elapsed"]),   years_run = c(rep(years, 3), years_4, years_4) ) %>%   mutate(     sec_per_year = round(seconds / years_run, 2),     relative = round(sec_per_year / min(sec_per_year), 1)   )  knitr::kable(runtime,              col.names = c("Scenario", "Seconds", "Years", "Sec/Year", "Relative to fastest"),              digits = 2,              caption = "Wall-clock time for simmar() by scenario. Sec/Year normalizes for different run lengths.")

title: Spatial Allocation Strategies
text: see five spatial allocation strategies side side, extract final-step effort across Scenarios 1–3 compare single figure.  can SSB see allocation strategy shapes resulting biomass landscape. rows correspond fleet allocation pairings (longline / handline), columns species.  patterns look across panels: rpue vs revenue: per-unit strategy (rpue) tends spread effort evenly, total strategy (revenue) concentrates effort high-biomass patches. Since neither accounts travel costs, effort extends freely remote patches. ppue vs profit: Adding travel costs shifts effort toward ports. per-unit version (ppue) especially reluctant send effort far port, creating biomass refugia remote areas. marginal_profit: Produces smoothest effort surface among cost-aware strategies, directly penalizes diminishing returns effort concentration. leads spatially uniform depletion. sole_owner vs open_access: spatial allocation (ppue), sole owner reaches equilibrium lower total effort higher biomass open access, retaining positive profits open access dissipates . classic MEY vs. open-access result, now playing spatially explicit setting. choice spatial_allocation can substantially change spatial footprint fishing, turn affects local depletion patterns, effectiveness spatial management tools like MPAs, economic outcomes fishery. See port-distance fleet-management vignettes fleet options interact marlin features.
code: extract_effort <- function(sim, fleet_name, label) {   final_step <- sim[[length(sim)]]   effort_vec <- final_step[[1]]$e_p_fl[, fleet_name]    expand_grid(x = 1:resolution[1], y = 1:resolution[2]) %>%     mutate(       effort = effort_vec,       strategy = label     ) }  all_efforts <- bind_rows(   extract_effort(sim_1, "longline", "rpue"),   extract_effort(sim_1, "handline", "revenue"),   extract_effort(sim_2, "longline", "ppue"),   extract_effort(sim_2, "handline", "profit"),   extract_effort(sim_3, "longline", "marginal_profit") ) %>%   mutate(strategy = factor(strategy,     levels = c("rpue", "revenue", "ppue", "profit", "marginal_profit")))  ggplot(all_efforts) +   geom_tile(aes(x, y, fill = effort)) +   geom_point(data = ports, aes(x, y),              color = "red", size = 2, shape = 17) +   scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "C") +   facet_wrap(~strategy, ncol = 3) +   coord_equal() +   labs(     title = "Effort Distribution by Spatial Allocation Strategy",     subtitle = "Red triangles = port locations",     fill = "Effort"   ) extract_ssb <- function(proc, longline_alloc, handline_alloc) {   last_step <- max(proc$fauna$step)    proc$fauna %>%     filter(step == last_step) %>%     group_by(critter, x, y) %>%     summarise(ssb = sum(ssb, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%     mutate(strategy = paste0(longline_alloc, " / ", handline_alloc)) }  all_ssb <- bind_rows(   extract_ssb(proc_1, "rpue", "revenue"),   extract_ssb(proc_2, "ppue", "profit"),   extract_ssb(proc_3, "marginal_profit", "rpue") )  ggplot(all_ssb) +   geom_tile(aes(x, y, fill = ssb)) +   geom_point(data = ports, aes(x, y),              color = "red", size = 1.5, shape = 17) +   scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "B") +   facet_grid(strategy ~ critter) +   coord_equal() +   labs(     title = "SSB by Allocation Strategy (longline / handline)",     subtitle = "Red triangles = port locations",     fill = "SSB"   )

title: Simulation of Marine Social-Ecological Systems
text: following example walks minimal one-fleet, one-species simulation introduce core marlin workflow. Key global parameters: resolution: grid dimensions. single integer creates square grid (e.g. resolution = 10 gives 10×10 grid). length-2 vector c(nx, ny) creates rectangular grid, resolution = c(2, 10) gives 2×10 grid. years: number years simulate. seasons: number seasons per year. 4 gives quarterly model, 12 monthly model, etc. patch_area: area patch km². marlin structured around three list objects: fauna: life histories species simulated fleets: fleet dynamics fishing behaviour manager: management strategies (MPAs, quotas, effort caps)
code: library(marlin) library(tidyverse) options(dplyr.summarise.inform = FALSE) theme_set(marlin::theme_marlin(base_size = 14))  resolution <- c(5, 10)   patch_area <- 2  years <- 20  seasons <- 4  time_step <- 1 / seasons  steps <- years * seasons

what: A note on FishLife default values

Important: FishLife values model estimates, direct measurements. Always verify retrieved values appropriate application, update local data possible. calling create_critter, check closest_taxa_match field returned object confirm FishLife matched intended taxon. recommend using scientific_name rather common_name. see error like Error seq.default(min_age, max_age, = time_step), typically network connectivity issue — simply re-running code usually resolves .

title: Simulation of Marine Social-Ecological Systems
text: start building fauna object using create_critter. arguments straightforward life history parameters. Two worth highlighting: adult_home_range recruit_home_range specify linear distance (km) within 95% adults recruits, respectively, expected disperse source patch course year.   fleets object list fishing fleets, created create_fleet. Every fleet contains one metier per species, specifying fleet’s interaction given critter: price per unit weight, selectivity, exploitation share. p_explt argument controls share total fishing mortality species attributed metier — values relative, absolute. two metiers p_explt = 1 p_explt = 2 species X, first accounts one-third total mortality second two-thirds.  tune_fleets calibrates catchability coefficients fleet achieves specified equilibrium depletion level. target 25% depletion, meaning fished biomass equals 25% unfished biomass equilibrium. calibration accounts fleet dynamics p_explt weights.  fauna fleets defined, pass simmar run simulation: process_marlin tidies raw simulation output data frames; plot_marlin provides quick visualisations:
code: fauna <-   list(     "bigeye" = create_critter(       common_name = "bigeye tuna",       adult_home_range = 5,       recruit_home_range = 15,       density_dependence = "local_habitat",       seasons = seasons,       depletion = .25,       resolution = resolution,       patch_area = patch_area,       steepness = 0.6,       ssb0 = 42,       m = 0.4     )   )  fauna$bigeye$plot() fauna$bigeye$plot_movement() fleets <- list(   "longline" = create_fleet(     list("bigeye" = Metier$new(       critter = fauna$bigeye,       price = 10,       sel_form = "logistic",       sel_start = 1,       sel_delta = .01,       catchability = 0,       p_explt = 1,     )),     base_effort = prod(resolution),     resolution = resolution   ) ) fleets$longline$metiers$bigeye$plot_selectivity() fleets <- tune_fleets(fauna, fleets, tune_type = "explt")  fleets$longline$metiers$bigeye$plot_catchability() start_time <- Sys.time()  example_sim <- simmar(   fauna = fauna,   fleets = fleets,   years = years )  Sys.time() - start_time #> Time difference of 0.04742098 secs # options(warn = 2)  processed_marlin <- process_marlin(sim = example_sim, time_step = time_step)  plot_marlin(processed_marlin) plot_marlin(processed_marlin, plot_var = "c", max_scale = FALSE) plot_marlin(processed_marlin, plot_var = "n", plot_type = "length", fauna = fauna) plot_marlin(processed_marlin, plot_var = "ssb", plot_type = "space", steps_to_plot = max(processed_marlin$fauna$step))

what: Two species, two fleets, with seasonal habitat shifts
title: Simulation of Marine Social-Ecological Systems
text: example extends simulation two species two fleets, adding seasonal habitat dynamics species occupies different spatial distribution depending time year.
code: seasons <- 4  steps <- years * seasons  time_step <- 1 / seasons  skipjack_home_range <- 2  bigeye_home_range <- 5  # Construct habitat quality surfaces as [ny, nx] matrices  skipjack_habitat <- expand_grid(x = 1:resolution[1], y = 1:resolution[2]) %>%   dplyr::mutate(     habitat = dnorm((x^2 + y^2), 20, 200),     habitat = habitat / max(habitat) * skipjack_home_range   ) |>   pivot_wider(names_from = x, values_from = habitat) %>%   select(-y) %>%   as.matrix()   bigeye_habitat <- expand_grid(x = 1:resolution[1], y = 1:resolution[2]) %>%   mutate(     habitat = dnorm((x^2 + y^2), 300, 100),     habitat = habitat / max(habitat) * bigeye_home_range   ) %>%   pivot_wider(names_from = x, values_from = habitat) %>%   select(-y) %>%   as.matrix()   bigeye_habitat2 <- expand_grid(x = 1:resolution[1], y = 1:resolution[2]) %>%   mutate(     habitat = dnorm((x^.2 + y^.2), 100, 100),     habitat = habitat / max(habitat) * bigeye_home_range   ) %>%   pivot_wider(names_from = x, values_from = habitat) %>%   select(-y) %>%   as.matrix()  a <- Sys.time()  fauna <-   list(     "skipjack" = create_critter(       scientific_name = "Katsuwonus pelamis",       habitat = list(skipjack_habitat, skipjack_habitat), # pass habitat as lists       season_blocks = list(c(1, 2), c(3, 4)), # seasons each habitat apply to       recruit_habitat = skipjack_habitat,       adult_home_range = skipjack_home_range, # standard deviation of the number of patches moved by adults       depletion = .6, # desired equilibrium depletion with fishing (1 = unfished, 0 = extinct),       density_dependence = "global_habitat", # recruitment form, where 1 implies local recruitment       seasons = seasons,       init_explt = 0.2,       explt_type = "f"     ),     "bigeye" = create_critter(       common_name = "bigeye tuna",       habitat = list(bigeye_habitat, bigeye_habitat2), # pass habitat as lists       season_blocks = list(c(1, 2), c(3, 4)), # seasons each habitat apply to       recruit_habitat = bigeye_habitat,       adult_home_range = bigeye_home_range,       depletion = .1,       density_dependence = "local_habitat",       seasons = seasons,       init_explt = 0.3,       explt_type = "f"     )   ) Sys.time() - a #> Time difference of 1.646941 secs  # Each fleet contains one metier per species. # price = price per unit weight; sel_form = "logistic" or "dome"   fauna$skipjack$plot_movement() fauna$bigeye$plot_movement() fleets <- list(   "longline" = create_fleet(     list(       "skipjack" = Metier$new(         critter = fauna$skipjack,         price = 100,         # price per unit weight         sel_form = "logistic",         # selectivity form, one of logistic or dome         sel_start = .3,         # percentage of length at maturity that selectivity starts         sel_delta = .1,         # additional percentage of sel_start where selectivity asymptotes         catchability = .01,         # overwritten by tune_fleet but can be set manually here         p_explt = 1       ),       "bigeye" = Metier$new(         critter = fauna$bigeye,         price = 10,         sel_form = "logistic",         sel_start = .1,         sel_delta = .01,         catchability = 0,         p_explt = 1       )     ),     base_effort = prod(resolution),     resolution = resolution   ),   "purseseine" = create_fleet(     list(       skipjack = Metier$new(         critter = fauna$skipjack,         price = 100,         sel_form = "logistic",         sel_start = 0.25,         sel_delta = .1,         catchability = .01,         p_explt = 0.9       ),       bigeye = Metier$new(         critter = fauna$bigeye,         price = 100,         sel_form = "logistic",         sel_start = .25,         sel_delta = .5,         catchability = .01,         p_explt = 1       )     ),     base_effort = prod(resolution), resolution = resolution   ) )  a <- Sys.time()  fleets <- tune_fleets(fauna, fleets)  Sys.time() - a #> Time difference of 0.194587 secs   # Run the simulation a <- Sys.time()  sim3 <- simmar(   fauna = fauna,   fleets = fleets,   years = years )  Sys.time() - a #> Time difference of 0.08545399 secs # a <- Sys.time()  processed_marlin <- process_marlin(sim = sim3, time_step = time_step, keep_age = TRUE) # Sys.time() - a  plot_marlin(processed_marlin) plot_marlin(processed_marlin, plot_var = "c") plot_marlin(processed_marlin, plot_var = "n", plot_type = "length", fauna = fauna)

title: Simulation of Marine Social-Ecological Systems
text: example compares effect identical MPA two species — yellowfin tuna shortfin mako shark — two spatial configurations. first scenario species share nearshore (eastern) habitat. second, tuna remain nearshore mako population centred offshore. MPA designed around tuna distribution cases, illustrating bycatch species’ unobserved range shift can determine whether MPA helps harms species.    define MPA data frame columns x, y, mpa, mpa = TRUE marks closed patches:  simulate MPA passing simmar via manager argument. mpa_year controls closure activated:  Now run offshore scenario: tuna habitat unchanged, mako population shifted offshore. apply exact MPA compare outcomes.
code: resolution <- c(20, 20) # resolution is in squared patches, so 20 implies a 20X20 system, i.e. 400 patches  seasons <- 1  years <- 100  tune_type <- "depletion"  steps <- years * seasons  yft_home_range <- 6  yft_depletion <- 0.6  mako_depletion <- 0.2  mako_home_range <- 5  yft_b0 <- 10000  mako_b0 <- 420  # Both species share the same nearshore (high-x) habitat in this first scenario  yft_habitat <- expand_grid(x = 1:resolution[1], y = 1:resolution[2]) %>%   mutate(     habitat = dnorm(x, resolution, 8),     habitat = habitat / max(habitat) * yft_home_range   ) %>%   pivot_wider(names_from = x, values_from = habitat) %>%   select(-y) %>%   as.matrix()   mako_habitat <- expand_grid(x = 1:resolution[1], y = 1:resolution[2]) %>%   mutate(     habitat = dnorm(x, resolution, 8),     habitat = habitat / max(habitat) * mako_home_range   ) %>%   pivot_wider(names_from = x, values_from = habitat) %>%   select(-y) %>%   as.matrix()   # Nearshore scenario: both species concentrated toward the eastern edge fauna <-   list(     "Yellowfin Tuna" = create_critter(       scientific_name = "Thunnus albacares",       habitat = yft_habitat, # pass habitat as lists       recruit_habitat = yft_habitat,       adult_home_range = yft_home_range, # cells per year       depletion = yft_depletion, # desired equilibrium depletion with fishing (1 = unfished, 0 = extinct),       density_dependence = "global_habitat", # recruitment form, where 1 implies local recruitment       seasons = seasons,       b0 = yft_b0     ),     "Shortfin Mako" = create_critter(       scientific_name = "Isurus oxyrinchus",       habitat = list(mako_habitat), # pass habitat as lists       recruit_habitat = mako_habitat,       adult_home_range = mako_home_range,       depletion = mako_depletion,       density_dependence = "global_habitat", # recruitment form, where 1 implies local recruitment       burn_years = 200,       seasons = seasons,       fec_form = "pups",       pups = 6,       b0 = mako_b0,       lorenzen_m = TRUE,       lorenzen_c = -0.25     )   )  fauna$`Shortfin Mako`$plot() fleets <- list("longline" = create_fleet(   list(     `Yellowfin Tuna` = Metier$new(       critter = fauna$`Yellowfin Tuna`,       price = 100, # price per unit weight       sel_form = "logistic", # selectivity form, one of logistic or dome       sel_start = .3, # percentage of length at maturity that selectivity starts       sel_delta = .1, # additional percentage of sel_start where selectivity asymptotes       catchability = .01, # overwritten by tune_fleet but can be set manually here       p_explt = 1     ),     `Shortfin Mako` = Metier$new(       critter = fauna$`Shortfin Mako`,       price = 10,       sel_form = "logistic",       sel_start = .1,       sel_delta = .01,       catchability = 0,       p_explt = 1     )   ),   mpa_response = "stay",   base_effort = prod(resolution),   resolution = resolution,   spatial_allocation = "rpue",   cost_per_unit_effort = 1,   effort_cost_exponent = 1.3 ))  a <- Sys.time()  # before <- fleets$longline$metiers$`Yellowfin Tuna`$catchability #  # fleets$longline$metiers$`Yellowfin Tuna`$spatial_catchability   fleets <- tune_fleets(fauna, fleets, tune_type = tune_type, tune_costs = TRUE,years = floor(years * .5)) # tunes the catchability by fleet to achieve target depletion  # fleets$longline$base_effort # after =  fleets$longline$metiers$`Yellowfin Tuna`$catchability   # fleets$longline$metiers[[1]]$catchability # fleets$longline$metiers[[1]]$catchability # fleets$longline$metiers[[2]]$catchability  # new_fleets$longline$base_effort # before == after  # fleets$longline$metiers$`Yellowfin Tuna`$spatial_catchability  Sys.time() - a #> Time difference of 1.805549 secs  # Run the nearshore baseline (no MPA) a <- Sys.time()  nearshore <- simmar(   fauna = fauna,   fleets = fleets,   years = years )  Sys.time() - a #> Time difference of 0.2482579 secs  proc_nearshore <- process_marlin(nearshore, time_step = fauna[[1]]$time_step)  plot_marlin(proc_nearshore, max_scale = FALSE, plot_var = "b") plot_marlin(proc_nearshore, max_scale = FALSE, plot_var = "ssb") set.seed(42) # specify some MPA locations mpa_locations <- expand_grid(x = 1:resolution[1], y = 1:resolution[2]) %>%   mutate(mpa = x > 15 & y < 15)  mpa_locations %>%   ggplot(aes(x, y, fill = mpa)) +   geom_tile() +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Accent", direction = -1, name = "MPA") +   scale_x_continuous(name = "Lon") +   scale_y_continuous(name = "Lat") a <- Sys.time()  nearshore_mpa <- simmar(   fauna = fauna,   fleets = fleets,   years = years,   manager = list(mpas = list(     locations = mpa_locations,     mpa_year = floor(years * .5)   )) )  Sys.time() - a #> Time difference of 0.324347 secs  proc_nearshore_mpa <- process_marlin(nearshore_mpa, time_step = fauna[[1]]$time_step)  plot_marlin(proc_nearshore_mpa, max_scale = FALSE) mako_habitat <- expand_grid(x = 1:resolution[1], y = 1:resolution[2]) %>%   mutate(     habitat = dnorm(x, .7 * resolution, 6),     habitat = habitat / max(habitat) * mako_home_range   ) %>%   pivot_wider(names_from = x, values_from = habitat) %>%   select(-y) %>%   as.matrix()   # Offshore scenario: same tuna habitat, mako now centred further from shore  fauna <-   list(     "Yellowfin Tuna" = create_critter(       scientific_name = "Thunnus albacares",       habitat = yft_habitat, # pass habitat as lists       recruit_habitat = yft_habitat,       adult_home_range = yft_home_range, # cells per year       depletion = yft_depletion, # desired equilibrium depletion with fishing (1 = unfished, 0 = extinct),       density_dependence = "global_habitat", # recruitment form, where 1 implies local recruitment       seasons = seasons,       b0 = yft_b0     ),     "Shortfin Mako" = create_critter(       scientific_name = "Isurus oxyrinchus",       habitat = list(mako_habitat), # Run the offshore baseline (no MPA) a <- Sys.time()  offshore <- simmar(   fauna = fauna,   fleets = new_fleets,   years = years )  Sys.time() - a #> Time difference of 0.2163219 secs  proc_offshore <- process_marlin(offshore, time_step = fauna[[1]]$time_step)  a <- Sys.time()  offshore_mpa_sim <- simmar(   fauna = fauna,   fleets = new_fleets,   years = years,   manager = list(mpas = list(     locations = mpa_locations,     mpa_year = floor(years * .5)   )) )  Sys.time() - a #> Time difference of 0.4657841 secs   proc_offshore_mpa <- process_marlin(offshore_mpa_sim, time_step = fauna[[1]]$time_step)  plot_marlin(proc_offshore) plot_marlin(proc_offshore_mpa) plot_marlin(   `MPA: Sharks Offshore` = proc_offshore_mpa,   `No MPA: Sharks Offshoe` = proc_offshore,   `No MPA: Sharks Nearshore` = proc_nearshore,   `MPA: Sharks Nearshore` = proc_nearshore_mpa,   steps_to_plot = NA,   plot_var = "ssb",   max_scale = FALSE ) plot_marlin(   `MPA: Sharks Offshore` = proc_offshore_mpa,   `Sharks Nearshore` = proc_nearshore,   `Sharks Offshore` = proc_offshore,   `MPA: Sharks Nearshore` = proc_nearshore_mpa,   plot_var = "ssb",   plot_type = "space",   steps_to_plot = c(years - 1) ) +   scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "Spawning Biomass", guide = guide_colorbar(barwidth = unit(13, "lines"), frame.colour = "black")) +   theme(     axis.text.x = element_blank(),     axis.text.y = element_blank(),     axis.title.x = element_blank(),     axis.title.y = element_blank(),     axis.ticks = element_blank(),     strip.text = element_text(size = 9)   )

what: De facto MPAs through bycatch penalties
title: Simulation of Marine Social-Ecological Systems
text: Fishing fleets can deterred areas without formal closures making certain patches unprofitable. example shows assigning large negative price shortfin mako — representing bycatch liability regulatory penalty — causes fleet avoid mako habitat, effectively creating de facto protected area.
code: years <- 100  tune_type <- "f"  # YFT habitat increases linearly with x (nearshore); mako is confined to the far corner (x>12, y>12)  yft_habitat <- expand_grid(x = 1:resolution[1], y = 1:resolution[2]) %>%   mutate(     habitat = .05 * x,     habitat = habitat / max(habitat) * yft_home_range   ) %>%   pivot_wider(names_from = x, values_from = habitat) %>%   select(-y) %>%   as.matrix()   mako_habitat <- expand_grid(x = 1:resolution[1], y = 1:resolution[2]) %>%   mutate(     habitat = x > 12 & y > 12,     habitat = habitat / max(habitat) * mako_home_range   ) %>%   pivot_wider(names_from = x, values_from = habitat) %>%   select(-y) %>%   as.matrix()   # Build fauna for the bycatch penalty scenario  fauna <-   list(     "Yellowfin Tuna" = create_critter(       scientific_name = "Thunnus albacares",       habitat = list(yft_habitat),       recruit_habitat = yft_habitat,       adult_home_range = yft_home_range,       depletion = .4,       density_dependence = "local_habitat", # recruitment form, where 1 implies local recruitment       seasons = seasons,       init_explt = 0.12,       explt_type = "f"     ),     "Shortfin Mako" = create_critter(       scientific_name = "Isurus oxyrinchus",       habitat = list(mako_habitat),       recruit_habitat = mako_habitat,       adult_home_range = mako_home_range,       depletion = .3,       density_dependence = "local_habitat", # recruitment form, where 1 implies local recruitment       burn_years = 200,       seasons = seasons,       init_explt = 0.1,       explt_type = "f"     )   )  # A large negative mako price makes patches where mako is present unprofitable, deterring effort  fleets <- list("longline" = create_fleet(   list(     `Yellowfin Tuna` = Metier$new(       critter = fauna$`Yellowfin Tuna`,       price = 100, # price per unit weight       sel_form = "logistic", # selectivity form, one of logistic or dome       sel_start = .3, # percentage of length at maturity that selectivity starts       sel_delta = .1, # additional percentage of sel_start where selectivity asymptotes       catchability = .01, # overwritten by tune_fleet but can be set manually here       p_explt = 1     ),     `Shortfin Mako` = Metier$new(       critter = fauna$`Shortfin Mako`,       price = -20000,       sel_form = "logistic",       sel_start = .1,       sel_delta = .01,       catchability = 0,       p_explt = 1     )   ),   mpa_response = "stay",   base_effort = prod(resolution),   resolution = resolution ))  a <- Sys.time()  fleets <- tune_fleets(fauna, fleets, tune_type = tune_type) # tunes the catchability by fleet to achieve target depletion  Sys.time() - a #> Time difference of 0.1189001 secs  # Run the simulation negative_prices <- simmar(   fauna = fauna,   fleets = fleets,   years = years )    proc_negative_prices <- process_marlin(negative_prices, time_step = fauna[[1]]$time_step) plot_marlin(   `De-Facto MPA` = proc_negative_prices,   plot_var = "ssb",   plot_type = "space" )

title: Allocate Catches, Revenue, and Profit by Fleet — allocate_yields
text: fishing-mortality share fleet patch-age cell given f_p_a_fl / f_p_a (already normalised). Total catch cell multiplied share give fleet-specific catch. Revenue calculated catch times price (p_p_a_fl). Profit revenue minus patch-level cost, costs follow formula: $$C_{l,p} = c_{0,l} \, E^{ref}_l \left[\left(\frac{E_{l,p}/n_{sp}}{E^{ref}_l}\right)^{\gamma_l} + \theta_l \, \tilde{d}_{l,p} \, \frac{E_{l,p}/n_{sp}}{E^{ref}_l}\right]$$ Note effort divided number species (\(n_{sp}\)) avoid double-counting costs across species loop simmar. internal helper called per critter per time step inside simmar go_fish.
